annotations.label: Regular surface is a Riemannian manifold and Riemann surface

anchor.snippet: Any regular surface is an example both of a Riemannian manifold and Riemann surface

annotations.note: Mathlib has `IsRiemannianManifold` as a general predicate, but no notion of regular surface in ℝ³ or the claim that one is both a Riemannian manifold and a Riemann surface.

annotations.label: Smooth map between Euclidean open sets

anchor.snippet: a map between two open subsets of Euclidean space is smooth if its partial derivatives of every order exist

annotations.note: Smoothness of maps between open subsets is `ContDiffOn 𝕜 ⊤`, defined for general normed spaces (Euclidean space included).

annotations.label: Regular surface

anchor.snippet: such that every point of S admits any of the following three concepts

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of a regular surface (local graphs/parametrizations/level sets) embedded in ℝ³.

annotations.label: Equivalence of the three definitions

anchor.snippet: The equivalence of all three definitions follows from the implicit function theorem

annotations.note: The implicit function theorem is formalized (`Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.Implicit`), but the equivalence of the regular-surface definitions is not, since regular surfaces are undefined.

annotations.label: Regular surface has smooth manifold structure

anchor.snippet: This shows that any regular surface naturally has the structure of a smooth manifold

annotations.note: Smooth-manifold infrastructure exists, but regular surfaces are not defined so this statement cannot be stated.

annotations.label: 2-sphere as a regular surface

anchor.snippet: A simple example of a regular surface is given by the 2-sphere

annotations.note: The unit n-sphere in an inner product space carries an analytic manifold structure via stereographic projection; the 2-sphere is the special case.

annotations.label: Torus of revolution

anchor.snippet: is a torus of revolution with radii r and R

annotations.note: The torus of revolution as an embedded regular surface in ℝ³ is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hyperboloid of two sheets

anchor.snippet: it can be covered by two Monge patches, with h

annotations.note: Monge patches and the hyperboloid as a regular surface are not formalized.

annotations.label: Helicoid

anchor.snippet: The helicoid appears in the theory of minimal surfaces

annotations.note: The helicoid surface is not formalized.

annotations.label: Tangent space

anchor.snippet: the tangent space or tangent plane to S at p

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the abstract manifold `TangentSpace`, not the embedded tangent plane spanned by f_u, f_v of a surface in ℝ³.

annotations.label: Normal space and unit normal vectors

anchor.snippet: The normal space to S at p

annotations.note: Orthogonal complements exist generally, but the normal space/unit normal of an embedded surface is not formalized.

annotations.label: Intrinsic definition of tangent vectors

anchor.snippet: an "intrinsic" definition of tangent vectors

annotations.note: Intrinsic tangent vectors as derivations of the algebra of smooth functions are formalized as `PointDerivation`, though the surface-specific equivalence-class definition is not.

annotations.label: Vector field as a derivation (Leibniz rule)

anchor.snippet: is a derivation , i.e. it satisfies the Leibniz rule

annotations.note: `Derivation`/`PointDerivation` encode the Leibniz rule on smooth functions, but the surface-specific directional-derivative-as-derivation statement is not separately formalized.

annotations.label: Lie bracket of vector fields

anchor.snippet: satisfies the Jacobi identity

annotations.note: The Lie bracket of vector fields on manifolds is defined as `mlieBracket` and the Jacobi identity is proven (`leibniz_identity_mlieBracket`).

annotations.label: Vector fields form a Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: form a Lie algebra under the Lie bracket

annotations.note: Antisymmetry and the Jacobi identity for the vector-field Lie bracket are proven, but the space of vector fields is not bundled as a `LieAlgebra` instance.

annotations.label: First and second fundamental forms

anchor.section: First and second fundamental forms, the shape operator, and the curvature
anchor.snippet: The first fundamental form depends only on f , and not on n

annotations.note: The first and second fundamental forms of a surface are not formalized.

annotations.label: Curvatures from the shape operator

anchor.section: First and second fundamental forms, the shape operator, and the curvature
anchor.snippet: the Gaussian curvature is the determinant of the shape operator

annotations.note: Gaussian/mean curvature and the shape operator are not formalized.

annotations.label: Positivity of E, G and EG−F²

anchor.section: First and second fundamental forms, the shape operator, and the curvature
anchor.snippet: are all necessarily positive

annotations.note: The first fundamental form coefficients E, G, EG−F² are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Effect of negating the unit normal

anchor.section: First and second fundamental forms, the shape operator, and the curvature
anchor.snippet: a negation of the choice of unit normal vector field will negate the second fundamental form

annotations.note: The second fundamental form and its dependence on normal orientation are not formalized.

annotations.label: Principal curvatures as extreme normal-section curvatures

anchor.section: First and second fundamental forms, the shape operator, and the curvature
anchor.snippet: are the maximum and minimum possible values of the curvature of this plane curve

annotations.note: Principal curvatures and normal sections are not formalized.

annotations.label: Christoffel symbols

anchor.section: Christoffel symbols, Gauss–Codazzi equations, and the Theorema Egregium
anchor.snippet: The Christoffel symbols assign, to each local parametrization

annotations.note: Christoffel symbols are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Christoffel symbols from the first fundamental form

anchor.section: Christoffel symbols, Gauss–Codazzi equations, and the Theorema Egregium
anchor.snippet: the Christoffel symbols can be calculated from the first fundamental form

annotations.note: Neither Christoffel symbols nor the first fundamental form are formalized.

annotations.label: Codazzi equations

anchor.section: Christoffel symbols, Gauss–Codazzi equations, and the Theorema Egregium
anchor.snippet: The Codazzi equations assert that

annotations.note: The Codazzi equations are not formalized.

annotations.label: Gauss equation

anchor.section: Christoffel symbols, Gauss–Codazzi equations, and the Theorema Egregium
anchor.snippet: The Gauss equation asserts that

annotations.note: The Gauss equation for surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Theorema Egregium

anchor.section: Christoffel symbols, Gauss–Codazzi equations, and the Theorema Egregium
anchor.snippet: This is known as the theorema egregium

annotations.note: The Theorema Egregium is not formalized (no Gaussian curvature).

annotations.label: Bonnet's fundamental theorem for embedded surfaces

anchor.section: Christoffel symbols, Gauss–Codazzi equations, and the Theorema Egregium
anchor.snippet: Pierre Bonnet proved that two quadratic forms satisfying the Gauss-Codazzi equations

annotations.note: Bonnet's fundamental theorem of surface theory is not formalized.

annotations.label: Isometry of surfaces

anchor.snippet: is said to be an isometry if it preserves the metric

annotations.note: Metric-space `Isometry` (distance-preserving maps) is formalized generally, but the surface isometry preserving the first fundamental form is not.

annotations.label: Length-preserving maps are isometries

anchor.snippet: preserves the lengths of all parametrized in curves

annotations.note: Path length exists (`pathELength`) but the theorem that length-preserving maps preserve the first fundamental form is not formalized.

annotations.label: Isometry from shared parametrization

anchor.snippet: A simple example of an isometry is provided by two parametrizations

annotations.note: This surface isometry example is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cylinder and plane locally isometric

anchor.snippet: The cylinder and the plane give examples of surfaces that are locally isometric

annotations.note: Local isometry of the cylinder and plane is not formalized.

annotations.label: Catenoid and helicoid locally isometric

anchor.snippet: the catenoid and helicoid are locally isometric

annotations.note: The catenoid–helicoid local isometry is not formalized.

annotations.label: Tangential vector field

anchor.snippet: A tangential vector field X on S assigns

annotations.note: Vector fields as sections of the tangent bundle are formalized, but not the embedded tangential vector field of a surface in ℝ³.

annotations.label: Smooth vector field

anchor.snippet: One says that X is smooth if the functions

annotations.note: Smoothness of vector fields is captured by `ContMDiffSection` of the tangent bundle, not via the surface-parametrization component condition.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative via Christoffel symbols

anchor.snippet: one defines ∇ Y X to be the tangent vector

annotations.note: Covariant derivatives (Koszul connections) on vector bundles are formalized, but not the surface definition via Christoffel symbols.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative via orthogonal projection

anchor.snippet: The orthogonal projection of this vector onto

annotations.note: Abstract covariant derivatives exist, but the construction as the tangential projection of the ambient derivative for embedded surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Covariant differentiation depends only on first fundamental form

anchor.snippet: covariant differentiation depends only on the first fundamental form

annotations.note: The intrinsic nature of covariant differentiation (Levi-Civita determined by the metric) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Surface of revolution

anchor.snippet: A surface of revolution is obtained by rotating a curve in the xz -plane

annotations.note: Surfaces of revolution are not formalized.

annotations.label: Regularity of a surface of revolution

anchor.snippet: will be a regular surface in ℝ 3

annotations.note: Regularity of surfaces of revolution is not formalized.

annotations.label: Rotationally symmetric minimal surfaces

anchor.snippet: any such surface is part of a plane or a scaled catenoid

annotations.note: Classification of rotationally symmetric minimal surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Geodesics on surfaces of revolution (Clairaut)

anchor.snippet: geodesics on S are governed by Clairaut's relation

annotations.note: Clairaut's relation for geodesics is not formalized (no geodesic theory).

annotations.label: Curvatures of a quadric surface

anchor.snippet: Consider the quadric surface defined by

annotations.note: Curvatures of quadric surfaces are not formalized.

annotations.label: Ruled surface

anchor.snippet: A ruled surface is one which can be generated by the motion of a straight line

annotations.note: Ruled surfaces are not formalized.

annotations.label: Vanishing Gaussian curvature of a ruled surface

anchor.snippet: The Gaussian curvature of the ruled surface vanishes if and only if

annotations.note: Gaussian curvature of ruled surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Vanishing Gaussian curvature characterizes developable surfaces

anchor.snippet: has vanishing Gaussian curvature near a point if and only if it is developable

annotations.note: Developable surfaces and this characterization are not formalized.

annotations.label: Minimal surface

anchor.snippet: Such a surface is called a minimal surface

annotations.note: Minimal surfaces are not formalized.

annotations.label: Meusnier: minimal iff mean curvature vanishes

anchor.snippet: A surface is minimal if and only if its mean curvature vanishes

annotations.note: Meusnier's characterization is not formalized (no mean curvature).

annotations.label: Douglas–Radó solution of Plateau's problem

anchor.snippet: gave an affirmative answer to Plateau's problem

annotations.note: Plateau's problem and its solution are not formalized.

annotations.label: Explicit minimal surfaces

anchor.snippet: Many explicit examples of minimal surface are known explicitly, such as the catenoid

annotations.note: Explicit minimal surfaces (catenoid, etc.) are not formalized.

annotations.label: Osserman: Gauss map of non-planar minimal surface is dense

anchor.snippet: if a minimal surface is non-planar, then its image under the Gauss map is dense

annotations.note: Osserman's theorem and the Gauss map are not formalized.

annotations.label: Surface of constant curvature

anchor.snippet: it is called a surface of constant curvature

annotations.note: Surfaces of constant Gaussian curvature are not formalized (no Gaussian curvature).

annotations.label: Unit sphere has curvature +1

anchor.snippet: The unit sphere in E 3 has constant Gaussian curvature +1

annotations.note: Gaussian curvature of the sphere is not formalized.

annotations.label: Plane and cylinder have curvature 0

anchor.snippet: The Euclidean plane and the cylinder both have constant Gaussian curvature 0

annotations.note: Gaussian curvature of plane/cylinder is not formalized.

annotations.label: Pseudosphere has curvature −1

anchor.snippet: A unit pseudosphere has constant Gaussian curvature -1

annotations.note: The pseudosphere and its curvature are not formalized.

annotations.label: Transitive symmetry groups of constant-curvature surfaces

anchor.snippet: The sphere, the plane and the hyperbolic plane have transitive Lie group of symmetries

annotations.note: SL(2,ℝ) acts transitively by isometries on the upper half-plane (`isPretransitiveSL2R`); the unified Lie-symmetry statement across all three geometries is not formalized.

annotations.label: Developable surfaces have curvature 0

anchor.snippet: Other examples of surfaces with Gaussian curvature 0 include cones , tangent developables

annotations.note: Developable surfaces and their curvature are not formalized.

annotations.label: Riemannian metric on a surface

anchor.snippet: In each local chart a Riemannian metric is given by smoothly assigning a 2×2 positive definite matrix to each point

annotations.note: A smoothly varying positive-definite inner product on tangent spaces is formalized as `RiemannianMetric`/`ContMDiffRiemannianMetric`, generalizing the 2×2 surface case.

annotations.label: Shape operator

anchor.snippet: the shape operator S x can be defined as a linear operator

annotations.note: The shape operator is not formalized.

annotations.label: Shape operator is self-adjoint

anchor.snippet: the shape operator is self-adjoint on the tangent space

annotations.note: Self-adjointness of the shape operator is not formalized.

annotations.label: Eigenvalues are the principal curvatures

anchor.snippet: The eigenvalues of S x are just the principal curvatures

annotations.note: Principal curvatures as eigenvalues of the shape operator are not formalized.

annotations.label: Determinant of shape operator is Gaussian curvature

anchor.snippet: the determinant of the shape operator at a point is the Gaussian curvature

annotations.note: Neither the shape operator nor Gaussian curvature are formalized.

annotations.label: Shape operator via unit normal field

anchor.snippet: Equivalently, the shape operator can be defined as a linear operator on tangent spaces

annotations.note: The shape operator defined via the unit normal field is not formalized.

annotations.label: Embeddability of analytic metrics

anchor.snippet: the theory of geodesics has been used to show this is true in the important case when the components of the metric are analytic

annotations.note: Local isometric embeddability of analytic metrics is not formalized.

annotations.label: Length and energy of a path

anchor.snippet: its length is defined by

annotations.note: Path length is formalized as `pathELength` (integral of the speed); the energy functional is not formalized.

annotations.label: Length independent of parametrization

anchor.snippet: The length is independent of the parametrization of a path

annotations.note: `pathELength` is proven invariant under monotone (and antitone) reparametrization.

annotations.label: Geodesic (Euler equations)

anchor.snippet: A path satisfying the Euler equations is called a geodesic

annotations.note: Geodesics defined via the Euler–Lagrange equations are not formalized.

annotations.label: Energy-minimizing path is a geodesic

anchor.snippet: a path minimising energy is just a geodesic parametrised by arc length

annotations.note: The energy-minimization characterization of geodesics is not formalized.

annotations.label: Geodesic curvature

anchor.snippet: The geodesic curvature k g at a point of a curve

annotations.note: Geodesic curvature is not formalized.

annotations.label: Geodesic curvature is intrinsic

anchor.snippet: The geodesic curvature at a point is an intrinsic invariant

annotations.note: Intrinsic invariance of geodesic curvature is not formalized.

annotations.label: Geodesic iff geodesic curvature vanishes

anchor.snippet: A unit speed curve on a surface is a geodesic if and only if its geodesic curvature vanishes

annotations.note: This characterization of geodesics is not formalized.

annotations.label: Geodesic iff acceleration is normal

anchor.snippet: is a geodesic if and only if its acceleration vector

annotations.note: The normal-acceleration characterization of geodesics is not formalized.

annotations.label: Gaussian curvature in orthogonal coordinates

anchor.snippet: then the Gaussian curvature is given by

annotations.note: The orthogonal-coordinate formula for Gaussian curvature is not formalized.

annotations.label: Gauss's derivative formula for the angle

anchor.snippet: The derivative of φ is given by a classical derivative formula of Gauss

annotations.note: Gauss's derivative formula for the angle is not formalized.

annotations.label: Exponential map

anchor.snippet: The exponential map is defined by

annotations.note: The Riemannian exponential map is not formalized (no geodesic flow).

annotations.label: Exponential map is a local diffeomorphism

anchor.snippet: and gives a diffeomorphism between a disc

annotations.note: Local diffeomorphism property of the exponential map is not formalized.

annotations.label: Determination of normal-coordinate Taylor series

anchor.snippet: this uniquely determines α , β , L , M , λ and μ

annotations.note: Normal coordinate Taylor expansions are not formalized.

annotations.label: Gauss lemma

anchor.snippet: usually called the Gauss lemma

annotations.note: The Riemannian-geometry Gauss lemma is not formalized (Mathlib's `GaussLemma` is about polynomial content).

annotations.label: Polar form of the metric

anchor.snippet: it follows that the metric has the form

annotations.note: The geodesic polar form of the metric is not formalized.

annotations.label: Geodesic convexity of small balls

anchor.snippet: under the exponential map is geodesically convex

annotations.note: Geodesic convexity of small metric balls is not formalized.

annotations.label: Theorema Egregium

anchor.snippet: shows that the Gaussian curvature of a surface can be computed solely in terms of the metric

annotations.note: The Theorema Egregium is not formalized.

annotations.label: Gauss–Jacobi equation

anchor.snippet: the Sturm–Liouville equation satisfied by

annotations.note: The Gauss–Jacobi (Jacobi field) equation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Gaussian curvature via Laplace–Beltrami operator

anchor.snippet: the Gaussian curvature at a point is given by the formula

annotations.note: The Laplace–Beltrami operator and this curvature formula are not formalized.

annotations.label: Isothermal coordinates

anchor.snippet: In isothermal coordinates , first considered by Gauss, the metric is required to be of the special form

annotations.note: Isothermal coordinates are not formalized.

annotations.label: Liouville's equation in isothermal coordinates

anchor.snippet: and φ satisfies Liouville's equation

annotations.note: Liouville's PDE for the conformal factor is not formalized.

annotations.label: Existence of isothermal coordinates

anchor.snippet: Isothermal coordinates are known to exist in a neighbourhood of any point on the surface

annotations.note: Local existence of isothermal coordinates is not formalized.

annotations.label: Gauss's geodesic triangle formula

anchor.snippet: if Δ is a geodesic triangle on a surface with angles

annotations.note: Gauss's angle-excess formula for geodesic triangles is not formalized.

annotations.label: Curvature as limit of angle excess over area

anchor.snippet: the curvature at a point can be calculated as the limit of angle excess

annotations.note: Curvature as a limit of angle excess over area is not formalized.

annotations.label: Gauss–Bonnet theorem

anchor.snippet: This is the celebrated Gauss–Bonnet theorem

annotations.note: The Gauss–Bonnet theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Poincaré–Hopf index theorem

anchor.snippet: the Poincaré-Hopf index theorem for vector fields on M

annotations.note: The Poincaré–Hopf index theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Positive curvature implies diffeomorphic to sphere

anchor.snippet: If the Gaussian curvature of a surface M is everywhere positive

annotations.note: This positive-curvature sphere result is not formalized (Poincaré-conjecture statements in Mathlib are `proof_wanted`).

annotations.label: Hilbert: embedded closed surface has positive-curvature point

anchor.snippet: Hilbert proved that every isometrically embedded closed surface must have a point of positive curvature

annotations.note: This theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Garsia conformal isometric embedding

anchor.snippet: as Adriano Garsia showed using the Beltrami equation

annotations.note: Garsia's conformal embedding result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Simply connected constant-curvature surfaces

anchor.snippet: The simply connected surfaces of constant curvature 0, +1 and –1

annotations.note: Classification of simply connected constant-curvature surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Dimension of moduli space of Riemann surfaces

anchor.snippet: has real dimension 6 g − 6

annotations.note: The 6g−6 moduli-space dimension is not formalized.

annotations.label: Poincaré uniformization theorem

anchor.snippet: By Poincaré's uniformization theorem , any orientable closed 2-manifold is conformally equivalent

annotations.note: The uniformization theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Euclidean cosine rule

anchor.snippet: the cosine rule for a triangle

annotations.note: The law of cosines is formalized as `EuclideanGeometry.law_cos`.

annotations.label: Flat tori embed in E⁴ but not E³

anchor.snippet: These closed surfaces have no isometric embeddings in E 3

annotations.note: Embeddability of flat tori is not formalized.

annotations.label: Isometry group of the sphere

anchor.snippet: The isometry group of the unit sphere S 2 in E 3 is the orthogonal group O(3)

annotations.note: The orthogonal group exists, but the identification of the sphere's isometry group with O(3) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Geodesics on the sphere are great circles

anchor.snippet: The geodesics between two points on the sphere are the great circle arcs

annotations.note: Spherical geodesics as great circles are not formalized.

annotations.label: Spherical cosine law

anchor.snippet: the spherical cosine law states that

annotations.note: The spherical law of cosines is not formalized.

annotations.label: Area of a spherical triangle

anchor.snippet: The area of the triangle is given by

annotations.note: Girard's spherical-excess area formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Rotations correspond to Möbius transformations

anchor.snippet: every rotation of S 2 corresponds to a Möbius transformation in SU(2)

annotations.note: The SU(2)/SO(3) correspondence with Möbius transformations of S² is not formalized.

annotations.label: Sphere unique closed orientable surface of curvature +1

anchor.snippet: The unit sphere is the unique closed orientable surface with constant curvature +1

annotations.note: This uniqueness statement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Real projective plane as a quotient

anchor.snippet: The quotient SO(3)/O(2) can be identified with the real projective plane

annotations.note: The identification SO(3)/O(2) ≅ ℝP² is not formalized.

annotations.label: Poincaré disk metric

anchor.snippet: be the Poincaré disk in the complex plane with Poincaré metric

annotations.note: The hyperbolic metric is formalized on the upper half-plane model, not on the Poincaré disk.

annotations.label: SU(1,1) acts transitively on the disk

anchor.snippet: acts transitively by Möbius transformations on D and the stabilizer subgroup of 0 is the rotation group

annotations.note: SL(2,ℝ) acts transitively by Möbius isometries on the upper half-plane; the conjugate SU(1,1)-on-disk version is not formalized.

annotations.label: Unique geodesic between two points of the disk

anchor.snippet: Any two points z , w in D are joined by a unique geodesic

annotations.note: Uniqueness of hyperbolic geodesics is not formalized (no geodesic theory).

annotations.label: Hyperbolic distance formula

anchor.snippet: The distance between z and w is given by

annotations.note: A closed-form hyperbolic distance formula (`dist_eq`) is proven for the upper half-plane, not the disk model.

annotations.label: Disk is complete as a metric space

anchor.snippet: as a metric space ( D , d ) is complete

annotations.note: The upper half-plane hyperbolic metric is proven to be a `ProperSpace` (hence complete), but for the half-plane model rather than the disk.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic cosine rule

anchor.snippet: the hyperbolic cosine rule states that

annotations.note: Hyperbolic cosh distance identities exist (`cosh_dist`), but the triangle cosine rule with angles is not formalized.

annotations.label: Area of a hyperbolic triangle

anchor.snippet: The area of the hyperbolic triangle is given by

annotations.note: The angle-defect area formula for hyperbolic triangles is not formalized.

annotations.label: Disk and half-plane conformally equivalent

anchor.snippet: are conformally equivalent by the Möbius transformations

annotations.note: Both the unit disc and upper half-plane are defined, but the conformal equivalence (Cayley transform) between them is not formalized.

annotations.label: Disk unique simply connected surface of curvature −1

anchor.snippet: The unit disk with the Poincaré metric is the unique simply connected oriented 2-dimensional Riemannian manifold with constant curvature −1

annotations.note: This uniqueness/uniformization statement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic surface examples

anchor.snippet: the Bolza surface of genus 2

annotations.note: Specific hyperbolic surfaces like the Bolza surface are not formalized.

annotations.label: Curvature under conformal change of metric

anchor.snippet: The new Gaussian curvature K′ is then given by

annotations.note: The conformal-change curvature transformation law is not formalized.

annotations.label: Liouville's equation for uniformization

anchor.snippet: it suffices to solve the following variant of Liouville's equation

annotations.note: The uniformization Liouville-type PDE is not formalized.

annotations.label: Solvability for negative Euler characteristic (Trudinger)

anchor.snippet: this non-linear equation can always be solved

annotations.note: Solvability of the uniformization PDE is not formalized.

annotations.label: Ricci flow proof of uniformization

anchor.snippet: The method of Ricci flow , developed by Richard S. Hamilton , gives another proof of existence

annotations.note: Ricci flow is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Riemannian (Levi-Civita) connection

anchor.snippet: The Riemannian connection or Levi-Civita connection

annotations.note: General covariant derivatives (and torsion via `CovariantDerivative.torsion`) are formalized, but not the metric-compatible torsion-free Levi-Civita connection specifically.

annotations.label: Parallel vector field along a curve

anchor.snippet: is said to be parallel along the curve if

annotations.note: Parallel transport along curves is not formalized (only the bundle covariant derivative exists).

annotations.label: Curvature from parallel transport around loops

anchor.snippet: The Gaussian curvature at a point can be recovered from parallel transport around increasingly small loops

annotations.note: Recovering curvature from holonomy around small loops is not formalized.

annotations.label: Kobayashi: connection 1-form as pullback of the sphere's

anchor.snippet: is just the pullback under the Gauss map of the connection 1-form on S 2

annotations.note: Kobayashi's pullback description of the connection 1-form is not formalized.

annotations.label: Klingenberg injectivity radius bound

anchor.snippet: Wilhelm Klingenberg proved in 1959 that the injectivity radius of a closed surface is bounded below

annotations.note: Injectivity radius and Klingenberg's bound are not formalized.

annotations.label: Bonnet diameter bound

anchor.snippet: the diameter of a closed surface of positive Gaussian curvature is always bounded above

annotations.note: The Bonnet (–Myers) diameter bound for surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cohn-Vossen rigidity of ovaloids

anchor.snippet: In 1927 Cohn-Vossen proved that two ovaloids

annotations.note: Cohn-Vossen rigidity is not formalized.

annotations.label: Liebmann: constant curvature closed surface is a sphere

anchor.snippet: a closed embedded surface of constant Gaussian curvature must be a sphere

annotations.note: Liebmann's theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hopf: constant mean curvature genus-0 surface is a sphere

anchor.snippet: Heinz Hopf showed in 1950 that a closed embedded surface with constant mean curvature and genus 0

annotations.note: Hopf's constant-mean-curvature sphere theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Carathéodory conjecture on umbilic points

anchor.snippet: a closed convex three times differentiable surface admits at least two umbilic points

annotations.note: The Carathéodory umbilic-points conjecture is not formalized.

annotations.label: Zero Gaussian curvature surfaces are cylinders or planes

anchor.snippet: a complete surface in E 3 with zero Gaussian curvature must be a cylinder or a plane

annotations.note: This flat-surface classification is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hilbert's theorem on constant negative curvature

anchor.snippet: no complete surface with constant negative curvature can be immersed isometrically in E 3

annotations.note: Hilbert's theorem on constant negative curvature is not formalized.

annotations.label: Willmore conjecture

anchor.snippet: the integral of the square of the mean curvature of a torus immersed in E 3 should be bounded below

annotations.note: The Willmore conjecture is not formalized.

annotations.label: Schmidt isoperimetric inequality on sphere and hyperbolic plane

anchor.snippet: In 1939 Schmidt proved that the classical isoperimetric inequality for curves in the Euclidean plane is also valid

annotations.note: The isoperimetric inequality on the sphere and hyperbolic plane is not formalized.

annotations.label: Loewner's torus systolic inequality

anchor.snippet: In 1949 Loewner proved a torus inequality for metrics on the torus

annotations.note: Loewner's systolic inequality is not formalized.

annotations.label: Pu's inequality for the projective plane

anchor.snippet: Pu's inequality for the real projective plane from 1952

annotations.note: Pu's systolic inequality for ℝP² is not formalized.
